article.title: CRM Automation for Local Service Businesses: The Simple Stack That Works
article.primaryKeyword: CRM automation for local service business

facebook.caption:
CRM Automation for Local Service Businesses: The Simple Stack That Works

Local service leads usually leak after the form, not before it. This guide breaks down the simple CRM handoff stack: capture the source, qualify the request, confirm fast, route the right work to a human, and report on what actually converts.

Read it: https://eb28.co/blog/crm-automation-for-local-service-businesses-the-simple-stack-that-works/

linkedin.caption:
Refreshed EB28 growth guide: CRM Automation for Local Service Businesses: The Simple Stack That Works

The useful CRM stack for local service businesses is not a bloated sales platform. It is a clean first-five-minute handoff: source tracking, qualification, confirmation, owner routing, reminders, and reporting that ties back to organic search intent.

https://eb28.co/blog/crm-automation-for-local-service-businesses-the-simple-stack-that-works/

instagram.caption:
CRM automation for local service businesses: fix the first five minutes after someone asks for help.

Capture the source. Qualify the request. Confirm fast. Route the right work to a human. Report what converted.

Full guide: eb28.co/blog/
instagram.creativeBrief: Carousel: Slide 1 first-five-minute lead leak, Slide 2 capture source and service need, Slide 3 qualify and route, Slide 4 confirm and remind, Slide 5 CTA to Melbourne Web Studio project brief.

shortFormVideo.hook: Your CRM automation should fix the first five minutes after a lead comes in.
shortFormVideo.script: Open on a form submission sitting without context. Show the simple stack: source URL, service need, urgency, confirmation, owner routing, reminders, and reporting. Cut to the warning: do not automate pricing judgment or trust-heavy exceptions. Close with the EB28 CRM automation guide and the Melbourne Web Studio project brief.
shotList: Website form submission with source page highlighted
Simple CRM pipeline columns: New, Qualified, Routed, Followed Up, Won/Lost
Checklist of what to automate versus what stays human
EB28 article URL and Melbourne Web Studio project-brief CTA
shortFormVideo.caption: Fix the first-five-minute handoff before adding more CRM automation.
